page: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 is a virtual machine instance from the Ddsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The configuration includes 64 physical cores and 128 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled. The processor is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

The instance is provisioned with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 7559 GB of local SSD storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. There are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this server configuration.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core processing, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached read operations also falls into the top 10% tier, reaching 10740.63 GB/sec and 658.72 GB/sec respectively. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Passmark memory scores placing in the middle 50% average. The system demonstrates high throughput for database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving, all scoring in the top 10%.

Given its high core count, substantial local storage, and top-tier multi-threaded performance, the D128ds_v7 is highly efficient for resource-dense, parallelized workloads. It is well-suited for large-scale database hosting, high-throughput web serving, and data compression tasks. The inclusion of AMX and AVX-512 instructions also makes it capable of handling CPU-based machine learning inference, though text generation speeds remain average. The qualitative cost profile reflects a high-density compute resource that trades off single-core efficiency for massive multi-threaded throughput and local storage capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 is a high-density virtual machine from the Ddsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 128-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of RAM and 7559 GB of local SSD storage, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance excels in multi-threaded workloads, with benchmarks showing top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, database operations, and static web serving. While single-core performance and memory latency scores are average, its support for Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets enables efficient CPU-based matrix math and cryptography. This server type is optimized for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and parallelized data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 is a 128-vCPU virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon processor, 512 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v7 is a 128-vCPU virtual machine with 512 GB RAM and 7559 GB local storage, optimized for high-throughput database workloads.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core virtual machine featuring massive local storage and advanced matrix extensions for parallelized enterprise workloads.

512 GB of system memory and 7559 GB of local SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance

Optimized for large-scale databases, web serving, and parallel processing
